purpose: Fable rulings and clarifications to carry into the O12 XBRL pin-amendment / Track-B read-view bundle. This file records decisions; it does not change experiment bars.

entries.principle: Driver birth source must not affect XBRL linking or materialization.
details: A Driver born from 8-K, transcript, news, 10-Q text, or 10-K text has the same XBRL-linking shape: no XBRL concept is carried at birth.
Resolution input is company + driver meaning + the company's filed PIT concept menu, not the driver's birth context.
Materialization is order-independent: du ids are functions of report id, driver, and fact_scope, not when the link landed.
A late active resolution backfills the same rows a day-one resolution would have written.
The only source-sensitive residue is Driver identity quality itself, which belongs to the admission/kernel experiments, not the XBRL linker.

entries.principle: A missing XBRL link at inference time is a safe under-attribution gap, not wrong data.
kept_before_link: surprise lane facts, which are text-born by design
company slice vocabulary from FS-14 menus
lost_until_link_backfill: full tagged series depth and regularity
exact XBRL values instead of rounded/as-stated text values
guaranteed per-period actual comparator for linked metric bases
entries.fail_closed_behavior: Before a link lands, read views fall back to available text facts and reported states; the gap self-heals through backfill once an active resolution is confirmed.

entries.rule: Read-time derived field only, never stored on Driver, never shown to producers.

derivation.active: An active ConceptResolution exists for the company + metric driver as of the read time.
derivation.revoked: A revoked ConceptResolution exists as of the read time; keep distinct from none because consumers should know a wrong-link event happened and facts may have been excluded.
derivation.none: No active or revoked ConceptResolution exists as of the read time. Abstained and never-attempted both read as none because the pinned store has no per-(company, driver) attempt log.

entries.pit_rule: In backtest/historical mode, derive as-of with the strict-< law: resolution date < as-of. In live/current mode, use current active/revoked state. State transitions (revocation / un-revoke RecoveryEvents) obey the same strict-< as-of law: the rendered status is the state as of that moment - a link active at as-of but revoked later reads active in that backtest.
notes: This mirrors the effective_driver_state idiom: read-derived, fail-closed, never written back.
This is awareness for consumers, not a gate on backfilled facts. P8d still permits backfill because values must exist inside each source filing.

entries.principle: Only strong-verified active ConceptResolutions may materialize XBRL facts.
details: No provisional link may write or materialize DriverUpdates.
The cheap pick before strong verification is invisible: it either becomes active after verify or becomes nothing.
A stored provisional/final distinction is rejected as over-engineering and wrong-data risk.
This follows P9 and P8a: materialization-grade resolution is mass-consequence and enrichment consumes active resolutions only.

entries.principle: Measure possible source-birth or thin-evidence bias without changing the linking design.
reporting_knobs: When resolution audits are parameterized, include a stratum for drivers resolved with no 10-K/10-Q text evidence yet or with thin evidence.
When X-XL2 twin_suspect calibration runs, report twin_suspect_rate split by driver birth source.

entries.principle: P4c gains one clarifying sentence: the explicit XBRL-to-canonical conversion factors, applied by the materializer at emit time - the text canonicalizer is never called on XBRL facts.

conversion_table.value: raw (per-share; per-X lives in the driver name per NAME-13/UNIT-08)

conversion_table.action: skip + count per class (P4c v1 whitelist)

riders: P4g dedupe/collision math runs on RAW values + RAW decimals BEFORE conversion; scaling happens at the emit step only.
Guard skip class usd_bare_pershare_suspect: per-share-looking concept qname with bare iso4217:USD -> skip + count.
X-XL0 verifier implements the same table independently (never imported from the materializer).
Unit names are keyed to live Unit.name spellings confirmed by the census unit inventory.
entries.classification: Implementation clarification of P4a/P4c. NOT a UNIT-11 or OD-10 amendment (text-side rules, untouched).

entries.principle: Binding (d): stored XBRL Period dates are exclusive-end (half-open storage). Every stored Period read decodes to effective human/reporting dates BEFORE any pinned rule runs.
rules: Duration: effective_start = start_date (unshifted); effective_end = end_date - 1 calendar day (date-component arithmetic, no timezone math).
Instant: effective_instant = stored date - 1 calendar day (start_date field; end_date='null' quirk).
P4b primacy, P14 classification, gp ids, build_known, comparator census, and the P4h null-pOR fallback (max EFFECTIVE duration end) all run on effective dates; raw dates stay in provenance.
True inclusive day count = raw end_date - raw start_date = effective_end - effective_start + 1; one shared helper, never double-adjust.
guards: Rows with raw end/instant == periodOfReport (would-be-inclusive signature) -> SKIP + count period_end_convention_suspect + per-fact log; never materialized (undecidable one-day windows must not mint gp ids).
Per-report tripwire: >=5 or >1% suspect current-period rows -> STOP for review (protects future refreshes/feeds).
report_primary_window_missing: reports with zero primary facts after decode -> count + WARN.
instant_off_pOR_by_one RETIRED - the +1 was the convention, not an anomaly.
entries.amendment_target: One clarification line for P4b / P14 / XBRLIntegrationDesign 5.3.
